description: Complete AgentHunt MVP: deploy to Render, verify Supabase connectivity, finalize launch artifacts, and ship launch commit

userStories.title: Add integration tests for MCP endpoint and HTTP API
userStories.description: As a developer, I need integration tests that verify the MCP endpoint and HTTP API work end-to-end with seeded data.
acceptanceCriteria: Test file tests/api.test.ts exists
Test verifies GET /api/health returns status ok
Test verifies GET /api/services returns array of services
Test verifies GET /api/services/:id returns a service with trust fields
Test verifies GET /api/services/:id/reviews returns reviews array
Test verifies GET /api/services/:id/trust returns verifiedInvocationCount and selfReportedInvocationCount
Test verifies POST /api/services creates a new service (with admin token if configured)
Test verifies POST /mcp with a valid JSON-RPC list_services call returns a valid response
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Add trust signal distinction to web UI
userStories.description: As a human browsing AgentHunt, I want to see verified vs self-reported invocation counts clearly distinguished on each service card and detail panel.
acceptanceCriteria: Service detail panel shows 'Verified invocations: N' and 'Self-reported: N' as separate labeled values
Service detail panel shows a trust label badge (verified-healthy, self-reported, watch)
The info grid in web/app.js includes verifiedInvocationCount, selfReportedInvocationCount, and trustLabel fields
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: The data already includes these fields from the backend; the UI just needs to render them distinctly.

userStories.title: Update README with production deployment instructions
userStories.description: As a developer or judge, I want the README to include the final production host URL, MCP usage example with curl, and clear setup instructions.
acceptanceCriteria: README.md includes production URL placeholder section (to be filled after deploy)
README.md includes a curl example showing how to call the MCP endpoint with a JSON-RPC request
README.md includes a curl example showing GET /api/services
README.md references llms.txt availability at /llms.txt
README.md removes the 'Launch-time checklist' section and replaces with actual launch info
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Update llms.txt with complete tool surface documentation
userStories.description: As an AI agent discovering AgentHunt, I want llms.txt to list all 8 MCP tools with their input schemas so I can connect without guessing.
acceptanceCriteria: llms.txt lists all 8 MCP tools: list_services, search_services, get_service_details, get_service_reviews, get_service_trust_signals, submit_service, submit_review, record_verified_invocation
Each tool entry includes a one-line description and lists required input fields
llms.txt includes the MCP endpoint path /mcp
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Verify Render deployment config and add health check
userStories.description: As an operator, I want render.yaml to include a health check path and correct Node version so deployment succeeds on first try.
acceptanceCriteria: render.yaml includes healthCheckPath: /api/health
render.yaml includes NODE_VERSION env var set to 22
package.json engines.node matches render config
Build succeeds with npm run build
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Create launch commit with Launch AgentHunt prefix
userStories.description: As a project maintainer, I want the final commit to use the reserved 'Launch AgentHunt' prefix as required by the PRD.
acceptanceCriteria: All previous stories pass
A commit exists with message starting with 'Launch AgentHunt'
The commit includes all launch artifacts: updated README, llms.txt, render.yaml
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: This is the final story. Only create this commit after all other stories pass.
